capability_objective: The stockroom lead is checking two inventory entries before replenishment planning. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the item named “Museum Display Hooks” at “Exhibit Storage” and the item named “Archival Tissue Paper” at “Conservation Lab”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Return only a two-row Markdown table with columns Record, ID, Date, and Status, preserving the requested order.
prompt:
The stockroom lead is checking two inventory entries before replenishment planning. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `inventory-audit.md` from full inventory records, not search summaries or backing files. Use the genuine local `./inventoryctl` executable through Pi's shell tool. Read `INVENTORY.md` and use the executable's built-in help to discover its interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the item named “Museum Display Hooks” at “Exhibit Storage” and the item named “Archival Tissue Paper” at “Conservation Lab”. Your first inventory-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./inventoryctl` processes, with no other inventory operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin a retrieval until both search results have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following inventory-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./inventoryctl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take ID, date, and status values only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter inventory data. Do not expose unrelated matches or extra full-record fields.

`inventory-audit.md` must contain only this Markdown table shape, with the two data rows in the requested order:
`| Record | ID | Date | Status |`
`|---|---|---|---|`
`| Museum Display Hooks (Exhibit Storage) | <stable-ID-or-unresolved> | <retrieved-date-or-unresolved> | <retrieved-status-or-unresolved> |`
`| Archival Tissue Paper (Conservation Lab) | <stable-ID-or-unresolved> | <retrieved-date-or-unresolved> | <retrieved-status-or-unresolved> |`

For a resolved branch, preserve the full-record ID, date, and status spelling exactly. For an unresolved branch, use the literal lowercase word `unresolved` in all three value cells. The two Status cells are the requested comparison; do not add a comparison sentence, heading, blank line, or other content. End the file with one newline. After writing it, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same table in your final response.
